perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Attachments A, G or H, neither party can suspend its further performance, terminate this Agreement or require specific performance of the other party of this Agreement in whole or in part as a result of the other party's material breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement without first providing notice to such party in writing and thirty (30) days' opportunity to cure the material breach (and then only if such party fails to cure such breach).
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Attachments A, G or H, neither party can suspend its further performance of this Agreement in whole or in part as a result of the other party's material breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement without first providing notice to such party in writing and thirty (30) days' opportunity to cure the material breach (and then only if such party fails to cure such breach).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "terminate this Agreement or require specific performance of the other party," the clause now contradicts section 17 of ATTACHMENT H, which gives a party the right to terminate the Agreement in case of any material breach. The removal makes it unclear if termination is still an option for breach, creating uncertainty about enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In case of any material breach of the terms and conditions contained in the Agreement by one of the parties, the other party may, without giving prior written notice in the event the material breach is not cured within such notice period or without undertaking any recourse to legal proceedings, suspend its further performance, terminate the Agreement or require specific performance by the other party of the Agreement in whole or in part, without prejudice to its right to damages for any losses incurred subject to Article 14.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the ability for a party to 'terminate this Agreement or require specific performance of the other party'. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'the other party may...terminate the Agreement or require specific performance by the other party'. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text limits the remedies available for a material breach, contradicting the original allowance of termination as a remedy.

perturbation.original_text: For each Metal Crate not received back at Global Pallet Services Limited (GPS) and where a physical inventory determines that such Metal Crate is not in the custody of Buyer/Buyer Affiliates, Buyer/Affiliates will have 30 days from end of the calendar year to reimburse ExxonMobil as stated below. For the purposes of this agreement, a Metal Crate is considered to be in undamaged condition if the base and all four sides are in working condition. Buyer/Buyer Affiliates agrees to pay for all repairs for damage to any Metal Crate, or to pay the replacement charge of $[*****] per Metal Crate for any Metal Crate that is either permanently lost or damaged beyond repair while in the custody of Buyer/Buyer Affiliates, and a replacement charge ($[*****] per door) for any removable door missing from Metal Crates collected at Buyer's /Buyer Affiliates' location.
perturbation.changed_text: For each Metal Crate not received back at Global Pallet Services Limited (GPS) and where a physical inventory determines that such Metal Crate is not in the custody of Buyer/Buyer Affiliates, Buyer/Affiliates will have 30 days from end of the calendar year to reimburse ExxonMobil as stated below. Buyer/Buyer Affiliates agrees to pay for all repairs for damage to any Metal Crate, or to pay the replacement charge of $[*****] per Metal Crate for any Metal Crate that is either permanently lost or damaged beyond repair while in the custody of Buyer/Buyer Affiliates, and a replacement charge ($[*****] per door) for any removable door missing from Metal Crates collected at Buyer's /Buyer Affiliates' location.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence "For the purposes of this agreement, a Metal Crate is considered to be in undamaged condition if the base and all four sides are in working condition.", the definition of 'undamaged condition' is removed. This leads to a contradiction because the rest of the section discusses the buyer's responsibility for Metal Crates, repair costs, replacement charges for damaged crates and missing doors, but there is no clear definition on what constitutes an undamaged crate. This creates an ambiguity on what is exactly being covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Attachment F documents Buyer's/Buyer Affiliates' responsibility for returnable metal crates ("Metal Crates") used in the supply of Butyl products from Seller/ExxonMobil Selling Affiliates ("ExxonMobil"). Buyer/Buyer Affiliates wishes to receive Butyl in Metal Crates and ExxonMobil is willing to supply Butyl in Metal Crates, subject to the following agreement. Agreement Buyer/Buyer Affiliates is responsible for the Metal Crates in their custody at the replacement value of the Metal Crates. Buyer's /Buyer Affiliates' custody begins when Metal Crates are loaded onto the delivering carrier at the ExxonMobil or third party warehouse and ends when Metal Crates are loaded onto the carrier for return to Global Pallet Services Limited (GPS) USA, Inc. Buyer/Buyer Affiliates must implement a system to ensure Metal Crates are not lost or damaged, and are returned in undamaged condition (normal wear and tear excepted). Buyer/Buyer Affiliates will supply to ExxonMobil upon request copies of any Bills of Lading needed to verify return shipments of Metal Crates. For each Metal Crate not received back at Global Pallet Services Limited (GPS) and where a physical inventory determines that such Metal Crate is not in the custody of Buyer/Buyer Affiliates, Buyer/Affiliates will have 30 days from end of the calendar year to reimburse ExxonMobil as stated below. Buyer/Buyer Affiliates agrees to pay for all repairs for damage to any Metal Crate, or to pay the replacement charge of $[*****] per Metal Crate for any Metal Crate that is either permanently lost or damaged beyond repair while in the custody of Buyer/Buyer Affiliates, and a replacement charge ($[*****] per door) for any removable door missing from Metal Crates collected at Buyer's /Buyer Affiliates' location. A Metal Crate shall be deemed to have been damaged beyond repair if the documented repair costs to restore the damaged Metal Crate to fit-for-fill condition would exceed $[*****]. In addition, if any of the charges imposed by Global Pallet Services Limited (GPS) under the agreement between the ExxonMobil and Global Pallet Service Limited (GPS) for damage to or loss of Metal Crates, are revised, the aforesaid amount reimbursable by Buyer/Buyer Affiliates shall automatically be revised in similar manner.
perturbation.justification: The omission of the sentence defining 'undamaged condition' from the original text creates a significant ambiguity. The contradicted text states, 'Buyer/Buyer Affiliates must implement a system to ensure Metal Crates are not lost or damaged, and are returned in undamaged condition (normal wear and tear excepted).' Without a clear definition of 'undamaged condition,' it becomes impossible to determine whether the buyer has met their obligation, leading to a direct conflict and unenforceability. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, as essential language was removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties represent, warrant and undertake to each other on a continuous basis that they shall comply with all applicable anti-bribery and anti- money laundering laws, rules, and regulations of any government, including the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and the applicable country legislation implementing the OECD Convention on Combating Bribery of Foreign Public Officials in International Business Transactions as such laws and regulations may be amended or updated from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties represent, warrant and undertake to each other on a continuous basis that they shall comply with all applicable anti- money laundering laws, rules, and regulations of any government as such laws and regulations may be amended or updated from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "anti-bribery laws" including specific reference to the "U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, and the applicable country legislation implementing the OECD Convention on Combating Bribery of Foreign Public Officials in International Business Transactions", the altered clause now stands in contradiction with section (b) Compliance With Law in ATTACHMENT G that refers to compliance to all aplicable laws, regulations decrees and judicial orders. By removing the anti-bribery obligation in the first place, makes it ambiguous whether the parties are actually agreeing to adhere to such legal requirements and create in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_location: ATTACHMENT G, Business Practices (b) Compliance With Law
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Compliance With Law. Each party agrees and will secure agreement by its subcontractors to comply with all applicable laws, regulations, decrees and judicial orders. Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement to the contrary, no provision shall be interpreted or applied so as to require any party to do, or refrain from doing, anything which would constitute a violation of, or result in a loss of economic benefit under, United States anti-boycott and other export laws and regulations. Each party represents to the other party that it shall not make any improper payments of money or anything of value to a government official (whether appointed, elected, honorary, or a career government employee) in connection with this Agreement, nor shall it make improper payments to a third party knowing or suspecting that the third party will give the payment, or a portion of it, to a government official.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'anti-bribery laws' and related references in the changed text creates a direct contradiction with the 'Compliance With Law' section. The original text explicitly includes compliance with anti-bribery laws, while the changed text removes this obligation. The 'Compliance With Law' section requires compliance with 'all applicable laws, regulations, decrees and judicial orders,' which, in the original context, included anti-bribery laws. By removing the explicit mention in the changed text, it creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction regarding the parties' obligation to comply with anti-bribery laws, thus contradicting the 'Compliance With Law' section.
